reasoning_model_1: Model 1 accurately generates the text content "小心！前方有陷阱，别过去。" (00:00-00:03). Regarding the specific instruction to add stress/weight (重音) to the word "小心" (00:00-00:01), the performance is lackluster. The delivery of "小心" is quite light, casual, and lacks the necessary intensity or auditory prominence to distinguish it as emphasized. The pitch and volume remain relatively flat and consistent with the rest of the sentence, failing to convey the urgency of a warning or the specific stylistic requirement of the prompt. While the speech is fluent and natural, it ignores the paralinguistic command.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 accurately generates the text content "小心！前方有陷阱，别过去。" (00:00-00:04). The model successfully executes the instruction to emphasize "小心" (00:00-00:01). The word is spoken with distinct force, lower pitch gravity, and increased intensity compared to the subsequent phrases. There is also a notable pause immediately after "小心", which further serves to highlight the word and creates a sense of urgency appropriate for a warning about traps. The contrast between the emphasized word and the rest of the sentence is clear and effective.
model_comparison: Both models achieve perfect content accuracy and good fluency. The deciding factor is the adherence to the specific paralinguistic instruction to emphasize (add weight to) the word "小心". Model 1 delivers the sentence in a somewhat flat, casual tone where "小心" blends in with the rest of the sentence, showing no significant signs of emphasis. Model 2, on the other hand, clearly distinguishes the target word through increased intensity, a heavier tone, and meaningful pausing, effectively capturing both the instruction and the context of the warning. Model 2 is superior in following the specific feature control command.
